title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders on Object Reference Change

statement.markdown: Implement a React memoized component that optimizes re-renders when an object reference changes. The component should display the string representation of the object. Provide a memoization function that can handle object references. Test cases will be provided to verify the implementation.
statement.notes: The memoization function should return a memoized value when an object reference changes.

constraints.description: Maximum size of the input object

hints.text: Use React's built-in memoization hook to optimize re-renders.

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders on Object Reference Change
editorial.intuition: The key to this problem is to use React's memoization hook to cache the results of the object string representation. This way, when the object reference changes, the memoized value will be updated, and the component will re-render with the new reference.
editorial.bruteForce: One way to solve this problem is to use a brute-force approach by recalculating the string representation of the object every time the component re-renders.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use React's memoization hook to cache the results of the object string representation.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: When the object reference changes, the memoized value will be updated, and the component will re-render with the new reference.
commonMistakes: Not updating the memoized value when the object reference changes
